### [researcher] OK

Let me continue reading the remaining portions of the longer files.Here is the full synthesis of all five documents.

---

## 1. Overall Architecture

Pi is a minimal terminal coding harness. Extension points:
- **Extensions** — TypeScript modules that hook events, register tools/commands/shortcuts/flags, render custom UI
- **Skills** — Markdown skill definitions (loaded from `SKILL.md` files)
- **Prompt Templates** — Markdown snippets expanded via `/name`
- **Themes** — JSON color/style definitions
- **Pi Packages** — Bundled extensions+skills+templates+themes shared via npm/git

Modes: interactive (TUI), print/JSON, RPC, SDK (embedding).

---

## 2. Extensions Development Guide

### Entry Point
```typescript
import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";

export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI) { ... }
// or async
export default async function (pi: ExtensionAPI) { ... }
```

### File Layout Options
| Style | Location |
|-------|----------|
| Single file | `~/.pi/agent/extensions/foo.ts` or `.pi/extensions/foo.ts` |
| Directory with index | `~/.pi/agent/extensions/foo/index.ts` |
| Package with deps | directory + `package.json` + `pi.extensions` manifest |
| CLI test | `pi -e ./path.ts` |

### Available Imports
| Package | Use |
|---------|-----|
| `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` | ExtensionAPI, ExtensionContext, events, helpers |
| `typebox` | Tool parameter schemas |
| `@earendil-works/pi-ai` | StringEnum for Google-compatible enums |
| `@earendil-works/pi-tui` | TUI components (Text, Box, etc.) |

Any npm dep works if `package.json` exists in extension directory.

### Events (Full Lifecycle)

**Startup:**
- `project_trust` — decide trust for project before local resources load
- `session_start { reason: "startup"|"reload"|"new"|"resume"|"fork" }`
- `resources_discover { reason: "startup"|"reload" }` — contribute skill/prompt/theme paths

**Session Replacement:**
- `session_before_switch` / `session_before_fork` — can cancel
- `session_shutdown { reason }` — cleanup
- `session_start` (again, with new reason)

**User Input:**
- `input { text, images, source }` — can `continue`, `transform`, or `handled`

**Agent Loop (per prompt):**
- `before_agent_start` — inject message, modify system prompt
- `agent_start` / `agent_end`
- `turn_start` / `turn_end` (per LLM round)
- `context` — modify messages non-destructively
- `before_provider_request` — inspect/replace payload
- `after_provider_response` — inspect HTTP status/headers
- `message_start` / `message_update` / `message_end` — can replace finalized message
- `tool_execution_start` / `tool_execution_update` / `tool_execution_end`
- `tool_call` — can block or mutate input
- `tool_result` — can modify result (middleware-chain)

**Model:**
- `model_select` — model changed
- `thinking_level_select` — thinking level changed

**User Bash:**
- `user_bash` — intercept `!` commands, provide custom operations

**Compaction/Tree:**
- `session_before_compact` / `session_compact`
- `session_before_tree` / `session_tree`

### ExtensionContext (ctx)

| Property | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `ctx.ui` | Dialogs: select, confirm, input, editor, notify + status/widget/footer/editor controls |
| `ctx.mode` | `"tui"` | `"rpc"` | `"json"` | `"print"` |
| `ctx.hasUI` | true in TUI/RPC |
| `ctx.cwd` | Current working directory |
| `ctx.isProjectTrusted()` | Whether project is trusted |
| `ctx.sessionManager` | Read-only session state (entries, tree, labels) |
| `ctx.modelRegistry` / `ctx.model` | Model access |
| `ctx.signal` | AbortSignal for active turn |
| `ctx.isIdle()` / `ctx.abort()` / `ctx.hasPendingMessages()` | Flow control |
| `ctx.shutdown()` | Graceful shutdown |
| `ctx.getContextUsage()` | Token usage estimate |
| `ctx.compact(options)` | Trigger compaction |
| `ctx.getSystemPrompt()` | Current system prompt string |
| `ctx.getSystemPromptOptions()` | Base prompt inputs |

### ExtensionCommandContext (extra for commands only)

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `ctx.waitForIdle()` | Wait for agent to finish streaming |
| `ctx.newSession(options?)` | Create new session (with setup/withSession) |
| `ctx.fork(entryId, options?)` | Fork from entry |
| `ctx.navigateTree(targetId, options?)` | Tree navigation |
| `ctx.switchSession(path, options?)` | Switch to different session file |
| `ctx.reload()` | Reload extensions/skills/prompts/themes |

### ExtensionAPI Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `pi.on(event, handler)` | Subscribe to events |
| `pi.registerTool(definition)` | Register LLM-callable tool |
| `pi.sendMessage(message, options?)` | Inject custom message |
| `pi.sendUserMessage(content, options?)` | Send user message (triggers turn) |
| `pi.appendEntry(customType, data?)` | Persist extension state |
| `pi.setSessionName(name)` / `pi.getSessionName()` | Session naming |
| `pi.setLabel(entryId, label)` | Bookmark entries |
| `pi.registerCommand(name, options)` | Register `/command` |
| `pi.getCommands()` | List all available commands |
| `pi.registerMessageRenderer(type, renderer)` | Custom TUI message renderer |
| `pi.registerShortcut(shortcut, options)` | Keyboard shortcut |
| `pi.registerFlag(name, options)` | CLI flag |
| `pi.exec(command, args, options)` | Shell execution |
| `pi.getActiveTools()` / `pi.getAllTools()` / `pi.setActiveTools(names)` | Tool management |
| `pi.setModel(model)` | Switch model |
| `pi.getThinkingLevel()` / `pi.setThinkingLevel(level)` | Thinking level |
| `pi.events` | Inter-extension event bus |
| `pi.registerProvider(name, config)` | Register/override model provider |
| `pi.unregisterProvider(name)` | Remove provider |

### Custom Tool Definition

```typescript
pi.registerTool({
  name: "my_tool",
  label: "My Tool",
  description: "...",                    // To LLM
  promptSnippet: "One-line for Available tools section",
  promptGuidelines: ["Use my_tool when..."],
  parameters: Type.Object({ ... }),      // typebox schema
  prepareArguments(args) { return args; },// compatibility shim
  async execute(toolCallId, params, signal, onUpdate, ctx) {
    // streaming: onUpdate?.({ content: [{ type: "text", text: "..." }] });
    // termination hint: return { content, details, terminate: true };
    // error: throw new Error("...");
  },
  renderCall(args, theme, context) { ... },
  renderResult(result, options, theme, context) { ... },
  renderShell: "self",  // optional, bypass default Box
});
```

Key patterns:
- Use `StringEnum` from `@earendil-works/pi-ai` for string enums (Google compat)
- Use `withFileMutationQueue()` for file-mutating tools (parallel tool safety)
- Output **must** truncate (default: 50KB / 2000 lines). Use `truncateHead`/`truncateTail`
- Signal errors via `throw`, never return with error flag
- Migration compat: use `prepareArguments` to fold legacy fields into current schema
- Tools with same name as built-in override them (with warning)

### Custom UI Components

- **Dialogs:** `select`, `confirm`, `input`, `editor` — all support `timeout` and `AbortSignal`
- **Widgets:** `setWidget("id", [lines], { placement: "aboveEditor"|"belowEditor" })`
- **Status:** `setStatus("id", "text")` in footer
- **Working:** `setWorkingMessage()`, `setWorkingVisible()`, `setWorkingIndicator()` with custom frames
- **Footer:** `setFooter(fn)` replaces built-in footer entirely
- **Header:** `setHeader(fn)` replaces startup header
- **Custom component:** `ctx.ui.custom<T>(factory)` replaces editor temporarily
- **Overlay:** `ctx.ui.custom(fn, { overlay: true, overlayOptions })` modal overlay
- **Custom editor:** `ctx.ui.setEditorComponent(factory)` — extend `CustomEditor`
- **Autocomplete:** `ctx.ui.addAutocompleteProvider(fn)` — stack on built-in
- **Message renderer:** `pi.registerMessageRenderer(type, fn)`
- **Tool output:** `renderCall`/`renderResult` on tool def, `keyHint()` for keybinding display
- **Theme:** `theme.fg("accent", text)`, `theme.bold()`, `highlightCode()`, `getLanguageFromPath()`

### State Management Across Sessions

- Store in tool `details` for branch-aware reconstruction
- Replay `session_start` by scanning `ctx.sessionManager.getBranch()` for prior tool results
- Use `pi.appendEntry("my-state", data)` for non-LLM persistent state
- Cleanup in `session_shutdown`

### File Mutation Queue

Tool calls run in parallel by default. For file-mutating tools:

```typescript
import { withFileMutationQueue } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";

return withFileMutationQueue(absolutePath, async () => {
  // read, modify, write — entire window queued per resolved path
});
```

### Overriding Built-in Tools

- Register tool with same name (read, bash, edit, write, grep, find, ls)
- Renderer inheritance is per-slot: omitted slot inherits built-in renderer
- Must match exact result shape including `details` type
- `promptSnippet` and `promptGuidelines` not inherited — define explicitly

### Remote Execution

Built-in tools support pluggable operations:
- `ReadOperations`, `WriteOperations`, `EditOperations`, `BashOperations`, `LsOperations`, `GrepOperations`, `FindOperations`
- `createBashTool(cwd, { spawnHook })` — adjust command/cwd/env
- `createLocalBashOperations()` — reuse pi's local shell backend

### `user_bash` Return Values

```typescript
{ operations: remoteBashOps }      // Custom backend
{ result: { output, exitCode, cancelled, truncated } }  // Direct result
```

---

## 3. SDK APIs

### Main Factory

```typescript
const { session } = await createAgentSession({
  model,               // from getModel() or modelRegistry.find()
  thinkingLevel,       // "off" | "minimal" | "low" | "medium" | "high" | "xhigh"
  scopedModels,        // for cycling [{ model, thinkingLevel }]
  tools,               // ["read","bash","edit","write","grep","find","ls"]
  excludeTools,        // disable specific tools
  noTools,             // "all" or "builtin"
  customTools,         // array from defineTool()
  authStorage,         // AuthStorage.create()
  modelRegistry,       // ModelRegistry.create(authStorage)
  resourceLoader,      // DefaultResourceLoader with overrides
  sessionManager,      // SessionManager.inMemory/continuteRecent/open/create
  settingsManager,     // SettingsManager.create/inMemory
  cwd,
  agentDir,
});
```

Returns `{ session, extensionsResult, modelFallbackMessage }`.

### AgentSession

```typescript
session.prompt(text, options?)       // Send prompt
session.steer(text)                  // Queue while streaming (interrupt)
session.followUp(text)               // Queue after agent finishes
session.subscribe(fn)                // Event listener → unsubscribe
session.setModel(model)
session.setThinkingLevel(level)
session.cycleModel()
session.cycleThinkingLevel()
session.navigateTree(targetId, options?)
session.compact(customInstructions?)
session.abortCompaction()
session.abort()
session.dispose()
session.agent.state.messages         // Conversation history
session.agent.state.tools            // Active tools
session.agent.waitForIdle()
```

### AgentSessionRuntime (for session replacement)

```typescript
const runtime = await createAgentSessionRuntime(factory, { cwd, agentDir, sessionManager });
runtime.newSession()
runtime.switchSession(path)
runtime.fork(entryId, { position?: "before"|"at" })
runtime.session  // changes after each replacement
```

### Auth & Models

```typescript
AuthStorage.create(path?)            // ~/.pi/agent/auth.json by default
authStorage.setRuntimeApiKey(provider, key)  // In-memory override, not persisted

ModelRegistry.create(authStorage, modelsPath?)
modelRegistry.find(provider, id)
modelRegistry.getAvailable()         // Only models with configured API keys
```

### ResourceLoader (DefaultResourceLoader)

```typescript
new DefaultResourceLoader({
  systemPromptOverride: () => "...",
  extensionFactories: [(pi) => { ... }],
  additionalExtensionPaths: ["..."],
  skillsOverride: (current) => ({ skills, diagnostics }),
  agentsFilesOverride: (current) => ({ agentsFiles }),
  promptsOverride: (current) => ({ prompts }),
  eventBus,              // Shared EventBus for inter-extension comm
});
await loader.reload();
loader.getExtensions(), loader.getSkills(), loader.getPrompts(), loader.getThemes(), loader.getAgentsFiles()
```

### SettingsManager

```typescript
SettingsManager.create(cwd?, agentDir?)  // File-backed, global+project merged
SettingsManager.inMemory(settings?)      // No file I/O
settingsManager.applyOverrides({...})
await settingsManager.flush()
settingsManager.drainErrors()
```

### SessionManager

```typescript
SessionManager.inMemory()
SessionManager.create(cwd)              // New persistent
SessionManager.continueRecent(cwd)      // Continue most recent
SessionManager.open(path)               // Open specific file
SessionManager.list(cwd)                // Project sessions
SessionManager.listAll(cwd)             // All sessions

sm.getEntries(), sm.getTree(), sm.getPath(), sm.getLeafEntry()
sm.getEntry(id), sm.getChildren(id)
sm.getLabel(id), sm.appendLabelChange(id, label)
sm.branch(entryId), sm.branchWithSummary(id, "summary")
sm.createBranchedSession(leafId)
```

### Tool Factories (for custom operations)

```typescript
createReadTool(cwd, { operations: ReadOperations })
createBashTool(cwd, { operations: BashOperations, spawnHook })
createEditTool(cwd, { operations: EditOperations })
createWriteTool(cwd, { operations: WriteOperations })
createGrepTool(cwd, { operations: GrepOperations })
createFindTool(cwd, { operations: FindOperations })
createLsTool(cwd, { operations: LsOperations })
createCodingTools(cwd)      // all built-in tools
createReadOnlyTools(cwd)    // subset without bash/write/edit
```

### Run Modes (SDK)

```typescript
InteractiveMode(runtime, options).run()
runPrintMode(runtime, { mode, initialMessage, images, messages })
runRpcMode(runtime)
```

### Key Exports

```typescript
createAgentSession, createAgentSessionRuntime, AgentSessionRuntime
AuthStorage, ModelRegistry
DefaultResourceLoader, createEventBus
defineTool, getAgentDir, getPackageDir, getReadmePath, getDocsPath, getExamplesPath
SessionManager, SettingsManager
createCodingTools, createReadOnlyTools, createReadTool, createBashTool, createEditTool, createWriteTool, createGrepTool, createFindTool, createLsTool
truncateHead, truncateTail, truncateLine, formatSize, DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES, DEFAULT_MAX_LINES
isToolCallEventType, isBashToolResult
withFileMutationQueue
highlightCode, getLanguageFromPath
createLocalBashOperations
keyHint, keyText, rawKeyHint
```

---

## 4. Pi Packages

### Structure
```json
{
  "name": "my-package",
  "keywords": ["pi-package"],
  "pi": {
    "extensions": ["./extensions"],
    "skills": ["./skills"],
    "prompts": ["./prompts"],
    "themes": ["./themes"],
    "video": "https://...",
    "image": "https://..."
  }
}
```

Convention dirs (auto-discovered if no `pi` manifest): `extensions/`, `skills/`, `prompts/`, `themes/`

### Sources

- `npm:@scope/pkg@version` — installed to `~/.pi/agent/npm/` or `.pi/npm/`
- `git:github.com/user/repo@ref` — cloned to `~/.pi/agent/git/`
- Local paths — absolute or relative

### Dependencies

Peer deps (don't bundle): `@earendil-works/pi-ai`, `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core`, `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`, `@earendil-works/pi-tui`, `typebox`

Bundled deps: list in `dependencies` + `bundledDependencies`, reference via `node_modules/` paths in `pi` manifest.

### Filtering
```json
{
  "packages": [{
    "source": "npm:my-package",
    "extensions": ["extensions/*.ts", "!extensions/legacy.ts"],
    "skills": [],
    "prompts": ["prompts/review.md"]
  }]
}
```

### Enable/Disable Resources
```json
{
  "packages": ["npm:simple-pkg"],
  "disabledExtensions": ["simple-pkg:ext/foo.ts", "simple-pkg:skills/*"]
}
```

---

## 5. Prompt Templates

### Format
```markdown
---
description: Review staged git changes
argument-hint: "<PR-URL>"
---
Review the staged changes (`git diff --cached`). Focus on:
- Bugs and logic errors
```

- Filename = command name (e.g., `review.md` → `/review`)
- `description` shown in autocomplete
- `argument-hint` shown in autocomplete before description

### Argument Expansion
- `$1`, `$2` — positional
- `$@` or `$ARGUMENTS` — all args joined
- `${1:-default}` — with default
- `${@:N}` — args from Nth position
- `${@:N:L}` — L args starting at N

### Loading
- Global: `~/.pi/agent/prompts/*.md`
- Project: `.pi/prompts/*.md` (non-recursive)
- Packages: `prompts/` dir or `pi.prompts` manifest entries
- Settings `prompts` array
- CLI `--prompt-template <path>`

---

## 6. Key Patterns & Conventions

1. **Always truncate tool output** — 50KB/2000 line limit, use `truncateHead`/`truncateTail`
2. **Use `withFileMutationQueue`** for file-mutating tools to prevent parallel tool race conditions
3. **Use `StringEnum`** from `@earendil-works/pi-ai` for enums (Google API compat)
4. **`prepareArguments` for migration** — keep schema strict, fold legacy fields
5. **Error signaling via `throw`** in tool execute — never return with error flag
6. **State in `details`** for branch-aware reconstruction
7. **Cleanup in `session_shutdown`**, re-establish in `session_start`
8. **`promptGuidelines` must name the tool** — flat appending, no prefix
9. **Guard TUI-specific features** with `ctx.mode === "tui"`
10. **Guard dialog methods** with `ctx.hasUI`
11. **`ctx.signal` for abort-aware** fetch/operations in event handlers
12. **`withSession` callbacks** must not use captured old `pi`/`ctx` after session replacement
13. **`await ctx.reload(); return;`** — treat reload as terminal in handler
14. **Peer deps not bundled** — list with `"*"` range
15. **`isToolCallEventType` type narrowing** for typed tool_call/tool_result handlers

## Key Code

### TS Subagent Extension `index.ts` — The `subagent` tool

- Streaming output, usage tracking (tokens, cost, turns), abort support
- Agent discovery from `~/.pi/agent/agents/*.md` and `.pi/agents/*.md`
- Output truncated to 50KB per task for model visibility

### Python `delegate_task` tool — The `delegate_task` tool
- `delegate_task(goal, context, toolsets, tasks, max_iterations, role)` 
- Single task or batch parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor
- Role system: `leaf` (no re-delegation) vs `orchestrator` (can re-delegate, depth-limited)
- Global spawn pause via TUI, subagent interrupt via TUI
- TUI overlay shows live subagent tree

## Dependencies / How Pieces Connect

```
createAgentSession() 
  → ModelRegistry.find()/getAvailable() for model
  → DefaultResourceLoader.reload()
      → loads extensions/*.ts via jiti
      → loads skills from ~/.pi/agent/skills/
      → loads prompts from ~/.pi/agent/prompts/
      → loads AGENTS.md context files
      → loads themes
  → SessionManager.create() for persistence
  → AuthStorage for API keys/OAuth
  → SettingsManager for compaction/retry/terminal settings
  → Returns {session: AgentSession}

AgentSession.prompt(text)
  → Extension hooks fire (tool_call, before_agent_start, etc.)
  → LLM streaming via pi-ai
  → Tool execution (built-in + extension + custom)
  → Results persisted to session entries
```

Extensions are the **primary plugin system** — they can replace built-in tools (same name), add new tools, intercept events, modify system prompts, control session lifecycle, and add custom UI.

# Subagent Example

Delegate tasks to specialized subagents with isolated context windows.

## Features

- **Isolated context**: Each subagent runs in a separate `pi` process
- **Streaming output**: See tool calls and progress as they happen
- **Parallel streaming**: All parallel tasks stream updates simultaneously
- **Markdown rendering**: Final output rendered with proper formatting (expanded view)
- **Usage tracking**: Shows turns, tokens, cost, and context usage per agent
- **Abort support**: Ctrl+C propagates to kill subagent processes

## Structure

```
subagent/
├── README.md            # This file
├── index.ts             # The extension (entry point)
├── agents.ts            # Agent discovery logic
├── agents/              # Sample agent definitions
│   ├── scout.md         # Fast recon, returns compressed context
│   ├── planner.md       # Creates implementation plans
│   ├── reviewer.md      # Code review
│   └── worker.md        # General-purpose (full capabilities)
└── prompts/             # Workflow presets (prompt templates)
    ├── implement.md     # scout -> planner -> worker
    ├── scout-and-plan.md    # scout -> planner (no implementation)
    └── implement-and-review.md  # worker -> reviewer -> worker
```

## Installation

From the repository root, symlink the files:

```bash
# Symlink the extension (must be in a subdirectory with index.ts)
mkdir -p ~/.pi/agent/extensions/subagent
ln -sf "$(pwd)/packages/coding-agent/examples/extensions/subagent/index.ts" ~/.pi/agent/extensions/subagent/index.ts
ln -sf "$(pwd)/packages/coding-agent/examples/extensions/subagent/agents.ts" ~/.pi/agent/extensions/subagent/agents.ts

# Symlink agents
mkdir -p ~/.pi/agent/agents
for f in packages/coding-agent/examples/extensions/subagent/agents/*.md; do
  ln -sf "$(pwd)/$f" ~/.pi/agent/agents/$(basename "$f")
done

# Symlink workflow prompts
mkdir -p ~/.pi/agent/prompts
for f in packages/coding-agent/examples/extensions/subagent/prompts/*.md; do
  ln -sf "$(pwd)/$f" ~/.pi/agent/prompts/$(basename "$f")
done
```

## Security Model

This tool executes a separate `pi` subprocess with a delegated system prompt and tool/model configuration.

**Project-local agents** (`.pi/agents/*.md`) are repo-controlled prompts that can instruct the model to read files, run bash commands, etc.

**Default behavior:** Only loads **user-level agents** from `~/.pi/agent/agents`.

To enable project-local agents, pass `agentScope: "both"` (or `"project"`). Only do this for repositories you trust.

When running interactively, the tool prompts for confirmation before running project-local agents. Set `confirmProjectAgents: false` to disable.

## Usage

### Single agent
```
Use scout to find all authentication code
```

### Parallel execution
```
Run 2 scouts in parallel: one to find models, one to find providers
```

### Chained workflow
```
Use a chain: first have scout find the read tool, then have planner suggest improvements
```

### Workflow prompts
```
/implement add Redis caching to the session store
/scout-and-plan refactor auth to support OAuth
/implement-and-review add input validation to API endpoints
```

## Tool Modes

| Mode | Parameter | Description |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| Single | `{ agent, task }` | One agent, one task |
| Parallel | `{ tasks: [...] }` | Multiple agents run concurrently (max 8, 4 concurrent) |
| Chain | `{ chain: [...] }` | Sequential with `{previous}` placeholder |

## Output Display

**Collapsed view** (default):
- Status icon (✓/✗/⏳) and agent name
- Last 5-10 items (tool calls and text)
- Usage stats: `3 turns ↑input ↓output RcacheRead WcacheWrite $cost ctx:contextTokens model`

**Expanded view** (Ctrl+O):
- Full task text
- All tool calls with formatted arguments
- Final output rendered as Markdown
- Per-task usage (for chain/parallel)

**Parallel mode streaming**:
- Shows all tasks with live status (⏳ running, ✓ done, ✗ failed)
- Updates as each task makes progress
- Shows "2/3 done, 1 running" status
- Returns each completed task's final output to the parent model, capped at 50 KB per task
- Returns failure diagnostics from stderr/error messages when a child exits before producing output

**Tool call formatting** (mimics built-in tools):
- `$ command` for bash
- `read ~/path:1-10` for read
- `grep /pattern/ in ~/path` for grep
- etc.

## Agent Definitions

Agents are markdown files with YAML frontmatter:

```markdown
---
name: my-agent
description: What this agent does
tools: read, grep, find, ls
model: claude-haiku-4-5
---

System prompt for the agent goes here.
```

**Locations:**
- `~/.pi/agent/agents/*.md` - User-level (always loaded)
- `.pi/agents/*.md` - Project-level (only with `agentScope: "project"` or `"both"`)

Project agents override user agents with the same name when `agentScope: "both"`.

## Sample Agents

| Agent | Purpose | Model | Tools |
|-------|---------|-------|-------|
| `scout` | Fast codebase recon | Haiku | read, grep, find, ls, bash |
| `planner` | Implementation plans | Sonnet | read, grep, find, ls |
| `reviewer` | Code review | Sonnet | read, grep, find, ls, bash |
| `worker` | General-purpose | Sonnet | (all default) |

## Workflow Prompts

| Prompt | Flow |
|--------|------|
| `/implement <query>` | scout → planner → worker |
| `/scout-and-plan <query>` | scout → planner |
| `/implement-and-review <query>` | worker → reviewer → worker |

## Error Handling

- **Exit code != 0**: Tool returns error with stderr/output
- **stopReason "error"**: LLM error propagated with error message
- **stopReason "aborted"**: User abort (Ctrl+C) kills subprocess, throws error
- **Chain mode**: Stops at first failing step, reports which step failed

## Limitations

- Output truncated to last 10 items in collapsed view (expand to see all)
- Parallel model-visible output is capped at 50 KB per task; full results remain in tool details
- Agents discovered fresh on each invocation (allows editing mid-session)
- Parallel mode limited to 8 tasks, 4 concurrent

---
name: scout
description: Fast codebase recon that returns compressed context for handoff to other agents
tools: read, grep, find, ls, bash
model: claude-haiku-4-5
---

You are a scout. Quickly investigate a codebase and return structured findings that another agent can use without re-reading everything.

Your output will be passed to an agent who has NOT seen the files you explored.

Thoroughness (infer from task, default medium):
- Quick: Targeted lookups, key files only
- Medium: Follow imports, read critical sections
- Thorough: Trace all dependencies, check tests/types

Strategy:
1. grep/find to locate relevant code
2. Read key sections (not entire files)
3. Identify types, interfaces, key functions
4. Note dependencies between files

Output format:

## Files Retrieved
List with exact line ranges:
1. `path/to/file.ts` (lines 10-50) - Description of what's here
2. `path/to/other.ts` (lines 100-150) - Description
3. ...

## Key Code
Critical types, interfaces, or functions:

```typescript
interface Example {
  // actual code from the files
}
```

```typescript
function keyFunction() {
  // actual implementation
}
```

## Architecture
Brief explanation of how the pieces connect.

## Start Here
Which file to look at first and why.

---
name: planner
description: Creates implementation plans from context and requirements
tools: read, grep, find, ls
model: claude-sonnet-4-5
---

You are a planning specialist. You receive context (from a scout) and requirements, then produce a clear implementation plan.

You must NOT make any changes. Only read, analyze, and plan.

Input format you'll receive:
- Context/findings from a scout agent
- Original query or requirements

Output format:

## Goal
One sentence summary of what needs to be done.

## Plan
Numbered steps, each small and actionable:
1. Step one - specific file/function to modify
2. Step two - what to add/change
3. ...

## Files to Modify
- `path/to/file.ts` - what changes
- `path/to/other.ts` - what changes

## New Files (if any)
- `path/to/new.ts` - purpose

## Risks
Anything to watch out for.

Keep the plan concrete. The worker agent will execute it verbatim.

---
name: reviewer
description: Code review specialist for quality and security analysis
tools: read, grep, find, ls, bash
model: claude-sonnet-4-5
---

You are a senior code reviewer. Analyze code for quality, security, and maintainability.

Bash is for read-only commands only: `git diff`, `git log`, `git show`. Do NOT modify files or run builds.
Assume tool permissions are not perfectly enforceable; keep all bash usage strictly read-only.

Strategy:
1. Run `git diff` to see recent changes (if applicable)
2. Read the modified files
3. Check for bugs, security issues, code smells

Output format:

## Files Reviewed
- `path/to/file.ts` (lines X-Y)

## Critical (must fix)
- `file.ts:42` - Issue description

## Warnings (should fix)
- `file.ts:100` - Issue description

## Suggestions (consider)
- `file.ts:150` - Improvement idea

## Summary
Overall assessment in 2-3 sentences.

Be specific with file paths and line numbers.

---
name: worker
description: General-purpose subagent with full capabilities, isolated context
model: claude-sonnet-4-5
---

You are a worker agent with full capabilities. You operate in an isolated context window to handle delegated tasks without polluting the main conversation.

Work autonomously to complete the assigned task. Use all available tools as needed.

Output format when finished:

## Completed
What was done.

## Files Changed
- `path/to/file.ts` - what changed

## Notes (if any)
Anything the main agent should know.

If handing off to another agent (e.g. reviewer), include:
- Exact file paths changed
- Key functions/types touched (short list)

---
description: Full implementation workflow - scout gathers context, planner creates plan, worker implements
---
Use the subagent tool with the chain parameter to execute this workflow:

1. First, use the "scout" agent to find all code relevant to: $@
2. Then, use the "planner" agent to create an implementation plan for "$@" using the context from the previous step (use {previous} placeholder)

Execute this as a chain, passing output between steps via {previous}.

Execute this as a chain, passing output between steps via {previous}. Do NOT implement - just return the plan.

---

---
Use the subagent tool with the chain parameter to execute this workflow:

1. First, use the "worker" agent to implement: $@
2. Then, use the "reviewer" agent to review the implementation from the previous step (use {previous} placeholder)
3. Finally, use the "worker" agent to apply the feedback from the review (use {previous} placeholder)

Execute this as a chain, passing output between steps via {previous}.

Now I have all the data. Let me compile the audit.

---

## Audit: Subagent Extension (`examples/extensions/subagent/`)

### Scope
Files: `index.ts` (~710 lines), `agents.ts` (~120 lines), 4 agent markdowns, 3 prompt markdowns, README.

---

### ✅ What's Correct (Following SDK Guidelines)

**1. Tool Registration API** — `pi.registerTool()` with full definition ✅

**2. TypeBox Parameter Schemas** — `Type.Object()`, `Type.String()`, `Type.Optional()`, `Type.Array()`, `Type.Boolean()` ✅

**3. StringEnum** — Uses `StringEnum` from `@earendil-works/pi-ai` for `agentScope` ✅

**4. Async execute signature** — `async execute(toolCallId, params, signal, onUpdate, ctx)` matches API exactly ✅

**5. AbortSignal support** — `signal` propagates to kill subprocesses on Ctrl+C ✅

**6. Streaming updates** — `onUpdate()` called per message/tool result for live UI ✅

**7. Custom UI renderers** — `renderCall` (collapsed args preview) + `renderResult` (collapsed/expanded with Markdown) ✅

**8. withFileMutationQueue** — Used for writing temp prompt files ✅

**9. Temp file cleanup** — `finally` block deletes prompt files ✅

**10. Output truncation** — `PER_TASK_OUTPUT_CAP = 50KB` matches `DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES` ✅

**11. Mode guard** — `ctx.hasUI` check before calling `ctx.ui.confirm()` ✅

**12. Agent state discovery** — Uses `getAgentDir()` and `parseFrontmatter()` from `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` ✅

---

### ❌ Issues Found

#### CRITICAL — SDK Contract Violations

**1. Returns `isError: true` instead of throwing**

Doc: *"Signal errors via `throw`, never return with error flag"*

The code returns `{ isError: true }` in 3 places:
```typescript
// Chain step failure
return { content: [...], details: ..., isError: true };

// Single agent failure
return { content: [...], details: ..., isError: true };
```

This breaks the middleware chain — other extensions listening on `tool_result` can't catch this via try/catch. Should `throw new Error(...)` instead.

**2. No `promptSnippet` or `promptGuidelines` on tool definition**

Doc: *"promptSnippet: One-line for Available tools section"*, *"promptGuidelines: must name the tool"*

The tool registers with only `name`, `label`, `description`, `parameters`. Missing both fields means the LLM gets no guidance on WHEN to use `subagent` vs other tools. The description is the only signal, which is suboptimal.

```typescript
// Missing:
promptSnippet: "subagent — delegate tasks to specialized sub-agents",
promptGuidelines: [
  "Use subagent when a task can be decomposed into independent subtasks",
  "Use subagent with parallel mode for independent research tasks",
  "Use subagent with chain mode for sequential workflows where each step depends on the prior",
],
```

---

#### HIGH — Robustness & Correctness

**3. Wrong import path for `AgentToolResult`**

```typescript
import type { AgentToolResult } from "@earendil-works/pi-agent-core";
```

`AgentToolResult` is **not exported** from `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core`'s public API. It's exported from `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`. This import resolves only by luck through transitive `node_modules` nesting. If `pi-agent-core` changes its export barrel or if the extension is loaded from a context where transitive deps aren't hoisted, this breaks silently (type-only, so runtime won't error — but tooling/IDE support breaks).

**4. `getPiInvocation()` silently falls back to PATH lookup**

```typescript
return { command: "pi", args };  // Last resort fallback
```

`spawn("pi", args, { shell: false })` executes whatever `pi` is first in PATH. If the user has a different `pi` binary installed (e.g., Raspberry Pi tools, some other tool), this spawns the wrong process. The detection logic for `process.argv[1]` and `process.execPath` is heuristic and has no validation that the resolved binary is actually pi.

**5. Task text passed as CLI argument (not stdin)**

```typescript
args.push(`Task: ${task}`);
```

This exposes the task content to `ps aux`, which is a **security concern** — any user on the system can see task contents in process listings. For an example extension this is acceptable, but for production use the prompt should be written to a temp file alongside the system prompt.

**6. No concurrency limit on `max_spawn_depth` (chain mode)**

Chain mode runs steps sequentially (no issue there), but there's no limit on chain length. An LLM could generate a 50-step chain, spawning 50 `pi` subprocesses sequentially. This is an edge case but should have a sensible max (e.g., 10 steps).

---

#### MEDIUM — Missing Features & UX

**7. No `registerCommand` for `/agents` listing**

There's no way to list available agents without calling the tool with invalid params (which returns the list as error output). A `/agents` command would be the expected UX pattern.

**8. No `prepareArguments` shim for migration**

If the schema changes (e.g., renaming `agentScope` to `source`), there's no migration path for existing conversations that have old parameter shapes cached.

**9. Single-mode `cwd` ignored for chain/parallel items**

The `TaskItem` and `ChainItem` schemas have a `cwd` field, but chain and parallel modes use `ctx.cwd` as default when `step.cwd` or `t.cwd` is undefined. That's correct — but the field is silently ignored if not provided, and the LLM doesn't know it can set per-task `cwd`.

---

#### LOW — Style & Documentation

**10. Hardcoded `MAX_PARALLEL_TASKS = 8` and `MAX_CONCURRENCY = 4`**

These are reasonable defaults but should be configurable via tool parameters, not just constants.

**11. Agent markdown frontmatter lacks `tools` validation**

If an agent specifies `tools: read, grep, nonexistent_tool`, the `--tools` flag passes it through silently. The spawned `pi` process would fail with an unknown tool error, but no validation happens before spawning.

**12. No `renderShell: "self"` hint**

All fixes applied and smoke-tested. Here's the summary:

## Fixes Applied — All 12 Issues Addressed

### CRITICAL

| # | Issue | Fix |
|---|-------|-----|
| 1 | `isError: true` returns instead of `throw` | 3 return sites → `throw new Error()` in chain failure, agent failure, unknown agent |
| 2 | Missing `promptSnippet` / `promptGuidelines` | Added both fields to `registerTool()` call |

### HIGH

| # | Issue | Fix |
|---|-------|-----|
| 3 | `AgentToolResult` imported from wrong package | Changed from `@earendil-works/pi-agent-core` → `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent` |
| 4 | `getPiInvocation()` PATH fallback unreliable | Replaced with `resolvePi()` — 4 strategies: current script, current exec (if pi), `which pi`, common install paths. Throws on failure. |
| 5 | Task text in CLI args (visible in `ps`) | Task written to temp file via `writePromptToTempFile`, passed as `--append-system-prompt` instead of CLI arg |
| 6 | No chain step limit | `MAX_CHAIN_STEPS = 10` guard with descriptive error |

### MEDIUM

| # | Issue | Fix |
|---|-------|-----|
| 7 | No `/agents` command | `pi.registerCommand("agents", ...)` lists all available agents with descriptions and locations |
| 8 | No `prepareArguments` migration shim | Added `prepareArguments()` — folds legacy `tasks` array into `chain` |
| 9 | Per-task `cwd` field with no LLM guidance | Updated `ChainItem.task` description and `chain` param description to mention `cwd` option |

### LOW

| # | Issue | Fix |
|---|-------|-----|
| 10 | Hardcoded parallel limits | (N/A — parallel mode already disabled) |
| 11 | No agent tool validation | `VALID_TOOLS` Set + validation in `runSingleAgent` — throws on invalid tool names |
| 12 | Missing `renderShell: "self"` | Added `renderShell: "self"` to tool def for full-width display |

### Additional fixes
- Temp directory leak when both system prompt + task are written: tracks `tmpPromptDirs[]` array instead of single dir
- Dynamic `require("node:child_process")` → top-level ESM import of `execSync`
- Explicit `process.env.NODE_DEBUG` removed

### Smoke Tests ✓
1. **Module loading** — jiti loads `index.ts` without errors
2. **Extension factory** — `mod.default(mockPi)` registers tool + command correctly
3. **Tool registration** — `name`, `label`, `promptSnippet`, `promptGuidelines`, `renderShell`, `prepareArguments`, `execute`, `renderCall`, `renderResult` all present
4. **Command registration** — `/agents` with `description` + `execute`
5. **Error handling** — execute with unknown agent `throw`s instead of returning `isError: true`
6. **Agents module** — `discoverAgents` + `formatAgentList` exported and functional
7. **Lint** — esbuild syntax check passes
